findings.title: Vacuous 'or True' assertion in D3 chrome_backend adversarial-lookalike test
findings.description: test_error_code_never_carries_adversarial_enum_lookalike_text contains `assert excinfo.value.error_code in BIND_ERROR_MESSAGES or True  # documents: fixed literal is a valid closed value`. The `or True` makes this assertion unconditionally pass regardless of the left operand -- it asserts nothing and would not fail even if `error_code` were NOT a member of BIND_ERROR_MESSAGES. It is inert documentation dressed as an assertion. This does not undermine the test's real security guarantee: the preceding `assert excinfo.value.error_code == "chrome_not_available"` (line ~396) and the `detail` assertion after it are the load-bearing checks, and I verified by reverting chrome_backend.py to its pre-D3 form that this test file's suite (including this test) goes red as expected. Recommend either removing the vacuous line or replacing it with a real assertion (e.g. `assert excinfo.value.error_code in BIND_ERROR_MESSAGES`).

findings.title: A3's Windows path-separator fix has no direct unit test; regression would only ever surface as a suppressed warning, never a failure
findings.description: The A3 fix normalizes `_key()`'s path separator (`.replace("\\", "/")`) so ALLOWLIST keys match on Windows. There is no dedicated unit test that feeds `_key()` a Windows-style (backslash) relative path and asserts the returned key uses forward slashes -- coverage of the fix is entirely incidental, via running the full `test_dead_code_advisory` gate, which is explicitly advisory/warn-only (`warnings.warn(...)`, 'No assertion on count') and which the commit message itself notes CI does not run with `-W error::UserWarning`. So even on a native Windows CI runner, a future regression of this exact separator bug would not fail any test -- it would only reappear as noisy warnings (previously-allowlisted findings falsely surfacing as 'new'). This is a real, if low-severity, coverage gap for a fix whose entire purpose was making a Windows-specific bug visible.

residual_risks: Two pre-existing (not introduced by this diff) test failures remain red in the suite and could be mistaken for regressions from this sweep: (1) tests/test_bind_channel_chrome_backend.py::TestRealChromeBrowserRunnerAvailable::test_available_when_binary_and_websocket_present fails on Windows because it hardcodes '/bin/ls' as a stand-in Chrome binary path -- confirmed present verbatim in the pre-D3 commit (a8e42f2e / c104dd95^), unrelated to the D3 security fixes. (2) tests/test_debt_registry_format.py::test_cross_reference_debt_comments_have_registry_entries fails on stale debt_registry.toml line references for webui_app/api/llm_diagnostics_api.py and webui_app/api/v1/pipeline.py -- confirmed present at the pre-sweep merge-base (667e5cce), outside D2/D3's touched files. Both are explicitly disclosed in the D2/D3 commit messages as pre-existing/out-of-scope, and I independently reproduced both by running the suite, confirming the disclosure is accurate.
